Admissions 2024-25

Begin your journey towards becoming a successful engineer. Applications are now open for undergraduate and postgraduate programs.

Placement Rate

Admission Process

Follow these simple steps to secure your admission at Jerusalem College of Engineering

1

Check Eligibility

Verify if you meet the eligibility criteria for your desired program

2

Apply Online

Complete the TNEA application or college application form

3

Counselling

Attend TNEA counselling or college-level counselling

4

Document Verification

Submit original documents for verification

5

Fee Payment

Pay the admission fee and complete enrollment

Important Dates

TNEA Application Opens
May 2024
TNEA Application Closes
June 15, 2024
Rank List Publication
July 2024
Counselling Begins
July 2024
Classes Commence
August 2024

*Dates are subject to change. Please check official TNEA website for latest updates.

Eligibility Criteria

Check if you meet the eligibility requirements for your desired program

Undergraduate Programs

B.E./B.Tech Eligibility

  • Must have passed HSC (10+2) with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics
  • Minimum 45% aggregate marks (40% for reserved categories)
  • Valid TNEA rank or equivalent entrance examination score
  • Age limit as per Anna University norms

Postgraduate Programs

M.E./MBA Eligibility

  • Bachelor's degree in relevant engineering discipline
  • Minimum 50% aggregate marks (45% for reserved categories)
  • Valid TANCET score for M.E. programs
  • MAT/CAT/TANCET score for MBA program

Fee Structure

Transparent fee structure as per government norms. Additional scholarships available.

ProgramAnnual FeeRemarks
B.E. / B.Tech₹75,000Per Annum (Govt. Quota)
B.E. / B.Tech₹1,25,000Per Annum (Management Quota)
M.E.₹50,000Per Annum
MBA₹70,000Per Annum

*Fees are subject to revision as per government notification

Scholarships & Financial Aid

We believe financial constraints should not hinder education. Explore available scholarships.

Government Scholarships

BC/MBC/SC/ST scholarships from State Government

Full tuition fee

Merit Scholarship

For students scoring above 95% in qualifying exam

50% tuition fee waiver

Sports Quota

For state/national level sports achievers

Up to 25% fee waiver

Economically Weaker Section

For students from low-income families

Based on family income
